text: Ŋaan ki Yisa kpaamm a bii mi ki bin yet sɔɔ, ki bia betib a bin tur bik na jeet ki wun di.

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed the lake and brought the boat to shore near the town of Gennesaret.
text: Ba tan nyii mɔkgbeŋŋir na ni ki doo kunkonn paak Genesaret tiŋ nna, ki bi lor bi burgbetir ki senn.

ref: But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their relatives and their own family.”
text: Ŋanne ki u betib a “Yennu sɔkinii ki kɔŋŋit nibaakir see u mɔŋ mɔŋ doo ni nan u tiauŋ niib boor ni, nan u mɔŋ mɔŋ ŋaak ni.”

ref: But when Herod heard about Jesus, he said, “This must be John! I had his head cut off, and now he has come back to life.”
text: Bat Herod ŋanin nba gbat yoonba ki u yet a “U tee Jɔɔnn wunba ki n daan te ki bi pot u yur, ŋaan ki u jen fiir be u manfoor nie.”

ref: After the apostles returned to Jesus, they told him everything they had done and taught.
text: Li poorpoe ki Yisa toomiinba maŋ ŋmat jen u boor ni ki tan yetɔ linba kur ki bi tun ki bia wann niib.

ref: The next Sabbath he taught in the synagogue. Many of the people who heard him were amazed and asked, “How can he do all this? Where did he get such wisdom and the power to work these miracles?
text: Ki foon daar ki u tan kɔɔ Yenjiantu diiuk ni a wun saa wann nibur nba din be leŋ na Yennu maan. Niib na nba gbat u wannu na ki li teen bi kura yaarlituk, ki bi boi bi leeb a “Yanboor lannpoe ki u mɔk mani? U teen nlee ki tuun bakitnauŋ toonjaana na? U la linba na kur lee?

ref: Isn't he the carpenter, the son of Mary? Aren't James, Joseph, Judas, and Simon his brothers? Don't his sisters still live here in our town?” The people were upset because of what he was doing.
text: Kanpinta na kaa na-aa? Mari bija na kaa-aa? Ŋɔɔ kaa tee Jems nan Josef nan Judas nan Simon yɔɔrɔ na-aa? U niipoob kaa be nna na-aa?” Ki bi yêenɔ.

ref: He told them, “You may take along a walking stick. But don't carry food or a traveling bag or any money.
text: ki u betib a “Yoo nba ki i saa, i daa jikit siar ki saa, see patdiakai kuukɔɔ. I daa dia jeeti, i bia daa dia sunsomm paka, i bia daa wuu likirii i jafaanba ni.

ref: If any place won't welcome you or listen to your message, leave and shake the dust from your feet as a warning to them.”
text: Li-i tee ki i baar doo nba ni, ki niib na ki gaari, ki bia ki gbiintir i mɔmaan, yin nyi doo maŋ ni, ki pit i taapant ni tant bir li doo ni, ki lin teen siarbiiuk ki turib.”

ref: Herodias had a grudge against John and wanted to kill him. But she could not do it
text: Linba na paak Herodias ji din nân Jɔɔnn, ki loon wun kpiu, ŋaan ki fiti, kimaan Herod mɔŋ mɔŋ din ki saki.

ref: because Herod was afraid of John and protected him. He knew that John was a good and holy man. Even though Herod was confused by what John said, he was glad to listen to him. And he often did.
text: Herod mun din tiin Jɔɔnn, kimaan u mi nan Jɔɔnn tee popeendaanɔ wunba tiin Yennu. Li paak ki u din tinn Jɔɔnn. Ŋaan Jɔɔnn nba mɔɔntir Yennu maan na, ki li ŋmat Herod yan ni bonchiann, ŋaan ki u lek gubint ki gbiintir u maan maŋ.

ref: I swear that I will give you as much as half of my kingdom, if you want it.”
text: ki tur bonpobik na mɔsonn ki betɔ a “N sa tura linba kur ki a loon, hali li-i lekii baar ki n tura n naan na bɔkir gbaa.”

ref: The girl left and asked her mother, “What do you think I should ask for?” Her mother answered, “The head of John the Baptist!”
text: Ŋanne ki bonpobik na nyii, ki saa boi u naa a “N tan sa yetɔ a n loon bee na?” Ki u naa jiin a “Betirɔ a a loon Jɔɔnn wunba wuur niib Yennu nyunwuru na yure.”

ref: The girl hurried back and told Herod, “Here and now on a serving plate I want the head of John the Baptist!”
text: Li yoo na ki bonpobik na ŋmat yiama ki saan Bat Herod boor ni ki betɔ a “N loon Jɔɔnn wunba wuur niib Yennu nyunwuru na yure ki a kpeen tatilaauk ni ki turin mɔtana.”

ref: Herod was very sorry for what he had said. But he did not want to break the promise he had made in front of his guests.
text: Ki linba na tur bat na parbiir bonchiann, ŋaan u ji din kan fit yêt bik maŋ nba loon linba na, kimaan u dɔŋ senn mɔsonn saamm maŋ boor niwa.

ref: At once he ordered a guard to cut off John's head there in prison.
text: Ŋanne ki u tun yupotitɔɔ li taakpaak ni, ki betɔ a wun jen nan Jɔɔnn yur turɔ. Ki yupotitɔɔ na saan dansarik ni ki saa pot Jɔɔnn yur,

ref: The guard put the head on a serving plate and took it to the girl. Then she gave it to her mother.
text: ki kpeenn tatilaauk ni, ki jen nann ki tan tur bonpobik maŋ, ki u mun jiir ki tur u naa.

ref: When John's followers learned that he had been killed, they took his body and put it in a tomb. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Matthew
text: Jɔɔnn poorpoweiteeb nba gbat yoonba, ki bi baar ki tan jii u gbanant ki pii. Yisa nba dinn jab tusa ŋaŋŋmu biaŋinba (Matiu

ref: But so many people were coming and going that Jesus and the apostles did not even have a chance to eat. Then Jesus said, “Let's go to a place where we can be alone and get some rest.”
text: Nigbouŋ lek din tut, ki siab baat ki siab ŋmat, ki Yisa nan u poorpoweiteeb kɔŋ yaak nan bin di jeet. Li paak ki u betib a “Ŋaant ki ti yaat saan muuk ni ti kuukɔɔ, ki foi waan.”

ref: They left in a boat for a place where they could be alone.
text: Ŋanne ki bi kɔɔ burgbetir ni ki poot saan muuk ni, bi kuukɔɔ.

ref: That evening the disciples came to Jesus and said, “This place is like a desert, and it's already late.
text: Yoo nba tan gar ki u poorpoweiteeb tan baar u boor ni ki yetɔ a “Yoo gara, ki nna na mun tee muuk nie.

ref: Jesus replied, “You give them something to eat.” But they asked him, “Don't you know it would take almost a year's wages to buy all of these people something to eat?”
text: Ŋanne ki u mun jiin yetib a “I mɔŋ-n turib jeet ki bin di.” Ki bi ŋmant boiɔ “A loon ki ti jii pɔŋ kobii ŋannlee ki saan daa jeet ki tan turib a bin di-ii?”

ref: Then Jesus said, “How much bread do you have? Go and see!” They found out and answered, “We have five small loaves of bread and two fish.”
text: Ki u boib a “I mɔk boroboro kuna ŋaŋŋae? I saan got man.” Ba saan got ki bann yoonba ki bi jen ki tan betɔ a “Li tee boroboro kuna ŋaŋŋmue nan janbis ŋannlee.”

ref: Jesus told his disciples to tell the people to sit down on the green grass.
text: Ŋanne ki u bet nibur maŋ a bin bɔkit kar mɔpaauŋ paak wora wora nna.

ref: They sat down in groups of
text: Ki bi bɔkit kobik kobik, nan piiŋŋmuŋmu, ki kar paabu paabu.

ref: All of them saw him and were terrified. But at this same time he said, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
text: kimaan bi kur lauwa; ki jaŋmaanii soorib bonchiann. Li taakpaak ni ki u yetib a “Cheen i para man. Li tee mine; i daa tiin jaŋmaanii mani,”

ref: Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples to give to the people. He also divided the two fish, so everyone could have some.
text: Ki u jii boroboro na, nan janii ŋannlee na, ŋaan yaat got sanpaapo, ki tur Yennu niipoouk, ŋaan ŋmit ŋmit boroboro kuna na ki tur u poorpoweiteeb a bin tor niib maŋ; ki u bia ŋmit ŋmit janii ŋannlee na, ki bi tor sɔɔ kura.

ref: After everyone had eaten all they wanted,
text: Ki sɔɔ kur dii gboo ki bia tenn.

ref: Jesus' disciples picked up twelve large baskets of leftover bread and fish.
text: Ki u poorpoweiteeb lakin jeet nba tenn na, ki li gbee kpanchubit piik nan ŋannlee.

ref: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into the boat and start back across to Bethsaida. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
text: Linba na poorpo ki Yisa te ki u poorpoweiteeb kɔɔ burgbetir na ni ki gar u tɔɔnn po ki doo mɔk-pootleer po, ki saan Betsaida; ŋaan ki ŋɔɔ biar a wun chab nibur maŋ.

ref: Then he told them goodbye and went up on the side of a mountain to pray.
text: Wa chab niib maŋ gbenn yoonba ki u doo jɔsiar paak ki mei Yennu.

ref: Later in the evening he was still there by himself, and the boat was somewhere in the middle of the lake.
text: Ki li din tan baar dajonyiɔk, ki burgbetir na daa be nyun sinsuuk ni, ŋaan ki u mun tan daa be jɔɔr maŋ paak u kuukɔɔ,

ref: He could see that the disciples were struggling hard, because they were rowing against the wind. Not long before morning, Jesus came toward them. He was walking on the water and was about to pass the boat.
text: ki tan la ki u poorpoweiteeb gbaa nan burgbetir kannu, kimaan bi din kan ki tookit wouŋo. Ŋanne ki u din fiir somm nyun paak ki baar bi boor ni, kojomɔɔk sanyafaar. Wa tan baar yoonba, ki u teen nan u yaa wun garib na.

ref: When the disciples saw Jesus walking on the water, they thought he was a ghost, and they started screaming.
text: Ki bi la ki u somm nyun paak, ki bi mi a li tee kpeemɔe; ki bi yikin sanpaapowa,

ref: He then got into the boat with them, and the wind died down. The disciples were completely confused.
text: ŋaan kɔɔ ki pukimm burgbetir maŋ ni; ki wouŋ na ji ŋmin soonn. Ki li bakitib bonchiann,

ref: Their minds were closed, and they could not understand the true meaning of the loaves of bread. Jesus Heals Sick People in Gennesaret (Matthew
text: kimaan bi daa din ki bann wa won jii boroboro na ki tun bakitnauŋ toonn biaŋinba na paaki. Bi dudukit daa din ki soorir fanu. Yisa nba din te ki baatib paak biaŋinba (Matiu

ref: As soon as they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus.
text: Ki li chinchiauŋ kur po niib nba la Yisa, ki bi bannɔ yiama,

ref: So they ran all over that part of the country to bring their sick people to him on mats. They brought them each time they heard where he was.
text: ki bi tiin saa tingbouŋ maŋ loka kur po ki jii bi baatib ki baat u boor ni. Ba tuu gbat ki u be siaminba kur po, bi din tuu jii bi baatib lianii paake ki saan namm u boor ni.

ref: In every village or farm or marketplace where Jesus went, the people brought their sick to him. They begged him to let them just touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
text: Wa tuu dɔkit doi nan digbanbis nba kura ni, ki niib bonchiann dia bi baatib ki tokiiɔ nan dai niwa ki barimɔ a bin lek sii u tiat mɔgbana, ki binba siir na kura la laafia.

ref: Some Pharisees and several teachers of the Law of Moses from Jerusalem came and gathered around Jesus.
text: Tɔn, ki Farisiinba nan sennu wannteeb din tan nyii Jerusalem ki baar Yisa boor.

ref: You are good at rejecting God's commands so that you can follow your own teachings!
text: Yimm, i lek yêt Yennu sennu weiu a yin dia i mɔŋ mɔŋ kaare;

ref: They noticed that some of his disciples ate without first washing their hands.
text: Ŋanne ki bi la ki u poorpoweitesiab ki waa bi yejamm kaar, kimaan bi jak jeet di, ŋaan daa ki nit bi nii.

ref: The Pharisees and many others obey the teachings of their ancestors. They always wash their hands in the proper way before eating.
text: (Kimaan Farisiinba nan Juu teeb nileeb kur din tuu ki gbia bi di jeet see ki bi nit bi nii fanu kaman nan bi yejamm kaar nba din want biaŋinba na.

ref: None of them will eat anything they buy in the market until it is washed. They also follow a lot of other teachings, such as washing cups, pitchers, and bowls.
text: Li-i tee ki bi daa siar daak ni, bi tuu ki di see ki bi tan wuur sinsinna ki fit lian di. Bi bia din tuu dia kaa bonchiann ki pukin nanba po. Bi din tuu dia kaar nba senn nyunyuŋmanii sokiru, nan bi boba sokiru, nan bi dundɔɔnii wuuru poe.)

ref: The Pharisees and teachers asked Jesus, “Why don't your disciples obey what our ancestors taught us to do? Why do they eat without washing their hands?”
text: Ki Farisiinba nan sennu wannteeb maŋ boi Yisa a “Bee ki a poorpoweiteeb na ki dia ti damm kaar nba ki ti yejamm din wannit na, ŋaan kpan gbia bi di jeet ki ki nit bi nii?”

ref: Jesus replied: You are nothing but show-offs! The prophet Isaiah was right when he wrote that God had said, “All of you praise me with your words, but you never really think about me.
text: Ki Yisa jiin yetib a “Yennu sɔkinii Aisaya nba din yet linba po na set tee mɔniie. I tee kpinkpannii damme kaman nan wa din sɔb biaŋinba na a ‘Yennu yet a Niib na teenin nibaakir bi mɔi ni kuukɔɔe, ŋaan bi para set fɔk nanin bonchiann.

ref: It is useless for you to worship me, when you teach rules made up by humans.”
text: Bi jiantirin yanne, kimaan bi jii nisaarik kaa ki want niib, ki yaa a li tee min Yennu sennue.’ ”

ref: You disobey God's commands in order to obey what humans have taught.
text: Ki Yisa ŋamm yet a “I bakin Yennu sennue ki bir siar poa, ŋaan sak nisaarii kaar nba want linba na.

ref: But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they own has been offered to God.
text: Ŋaan yimm ŋanin yaa a nirɔ-i bet u naa amii u baa a ‘Ma bo sa turi linba na, li kpant Yennu yara;’

ref: You won't let those people help their parents.
text: a u ji mɔk yaak ki kan somm u naa amii u baa.

ref: And you ignore God's commands in order to follow your own teaching. You do a lot of other things just as bad. What Really Makes People Unclean (Matthew
text: Ya want niib kaar maŋ biaŋinba na, i lek yêen Yennu bariie. I bia tee bonsia bonchiann ki li naan nan linba na booru.” Linba be nisaarik par ni labaar (Matiu

ref: Then Jesus said: What comes from your heart is what makes you unclean.
text: Ki u bia ŋamm yet pukin a “Linba set nyi nisaarik par nie teenɔ jakint,

ref: Jesus called the crowd together again and said, “Pay attention and try to understand what I mean.
text: Ŋanne ki Yisa ŋamm din yiin niburchiauŋ na u boor ni, ki tan betib a “Yimm nba kur be nna na, gbiintir man ki bann ma yaa n pak linba na paak.

ref: After Jesus and his disciples had left the crowd and gone into the house, they asked him what these sayings meant.
text: Wa betib nna gbenn yoonba ki u nyik niib maŋ, ŋaan saan kɔɔ ŋasiak nna ni. Ki u poorpoweiteeb boiɔ a wun wannib barii maŋ paak.

ref: He answered, “Don't you know what I am talking about by now? You surely know that the food you put into your mouth cannot make you unclean.
text: Ŋanne ki u boib, “I mun bia lek daa tee yankpeenaee? I ki mi a linba kur ki nisaarik di, li ki teenɔ jakintii?

ref: It doesn't go into your heart, but into your stomach, and then out of your body.” By saying this, Jesus meant that all foods were fit to eat.
text: kimaan li ki kɔɔ u par ni kaa, ŋaan u poor nie ki li kɔɔ, ki bia kpant bint ki nyi.”

ref: unfaithfulness in marriage, greed, meanness, deceit, indecency, envy, insults, pride, and foolishness.
text: nan bonchonchonn toona, nan nianchiauŋ nan biitbooru booru nan faak faaru, nan toon-yana, nan funfunn, nan sanjaauŋ, nan karinbaanii, nan jat toona.

ref: All of these come from your heart, and they are what make you unfit to worship God. A Woman's Faith (Matthew
text: Linba na kure nyi nirɔ par ni ki teenɔ jakint u par ni.” Poosɔɔ yada labaar (Matiu

ref: The woman replied, “Lord, even puppies eat the crumbs that children drop from the table.”
text: Ki poo na jiin a “N Yomdaanɔ, li set tee mɔniie, ŋaan bɔi nba tuu dɔɔ teebur lɔuŋ ni na mun ganii waas jebura nba baa na.”

ref: Jesus left and went to the region near the town of Tyre, where he stayed in someone's home. He did not want people to know he was there, but they found out anyway.
text: Linba na poorpo ki Yisa nyii leŋ ki saan Taya nan Saidon bɔkir po, ki kɔɔ ŋasiak nna ni, ki ki loon sɔɔ-n bann nan u be leŋi; ŋaan u din kan fit bɔr u mɔŋi.

ref: A woman whose daughter had an evil spirit in her heard where Jesus was. And at once she came and knelt down at his feet.
text: Ki poosɔɔ nba ki narinbiiuk din be u bonpobik ni na gbat u po, ki u baar u boor ni ki tan gbaan u tɔɔnn ni.

ref: The woman was Greek and had been born in the part of Syria known as Phoenicia. She begged Jesus to force the demon out of her daughter.
text: Poo maŋ din ki tee Juu nirɔ kaa; bi din marɔ Fonisia tiŋ nie, Siria tingbouŋ ni. Ki poo na barin Yisa a wun nyinn narinbiiuk na u bonpobik maŋ ni.

ref: But Jesus said, “The children must first be fed! It isn't right to take away their food and feed it to dogs.”
text: Ŋanne ki Yisa betɔ a “Ŋaant ki ti dinn waas bi jeet sinsinn, kimaan li ki ŋan ki nirɔ-n jii waas jeet ki tur bɔi.”

ref: Jesus answered, “That's true! You may go now. The demon has left your daughter.”
text: Ki Yisa betɔ a “A jiinu nba na paak, i kun; a sa saa baar sɔɔ ki narinbiiuk na nyii a bonpobik na niwa.”

ref: When the woman got back home, she found her child lying on the bed. The demon had gone. Jesus Heals a Man Who Was Deaf and Could Hardly Talk
text: Ki poo maŋ set din fiir yaat kun ki saa baar ki la ki u bik na dɔɔ u dindɔɔnu paak, ki narinbiiuk na set nyiiwa. Yisa nba din te ki muuk muut biaŋinba

ref: Jesus left the region around Tyre and went by way of Sidon toward Lake Galilee. He went through the land near the ten cities known as Decapolis.
text: Tɔn, ki Yisa ji din nyik Taya yakir na, ŋaan tɔkin nyii Saidon yakir, nan tiŋ nba ki bi din yi Doi-piik na mɔkmɔk ki saa baar Galilee mɔkgbeŋŋir ni.
